meta.name: Garlic Butter Steak
meta.subtitle: Pan-seared steak basted with garlic, herbs, and butter (Herb & Garlic style)

source.name: ssa.cuisines.herb_garlic
notes: Canonical SSA Herb & Garlic baseline garlic-butter steak.
Designed for deterministic doneness targets and equipment-aware cooking (cast iron preferred).

yield.notes: Serves 4 when slicing steak and serving with sides; 2 if serving large portions.

required.label: Cast iron or heavy skillet
required.notes: Best for searing. Stainless also works. Nonstick is not recommended for high-heat sear.

ingredients.label: Steaks (ribeye or strip), 1-inch thick
notes: Thickness drives timing; adjust for thinner/thicker cuts.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Salt generously. For best results, salt 30–60 minutes ahead (dry brine).

ingredients.label: High-heat oil (avocado, grapeseed, or light olive oil)
Garlic cloves, lightly smashed
notes: Smashed garlic perfumes butter without burning as quickly as minced garlic.

ingredients.label: Fresh thyme sprigs
notes: If you only use one herb, thyme is the most forgiving.

ingredients.label: Lemon wedges (optional)
notes: A squeeze at the end brightens the butter.

steps.title: Prep and season steak
instructions: Pat steaks dry thoroughly with paper towels.
Season both sides with kosher salt and black pepper.
If time allows, rest steaks uncovered 30–60 minutes at room temp (or 2–24 hours in the fridge for a dry brine).

steps.title: Preheat the pan
instructions: Heat a cast iron or heavy skillet over medium-high until very hot (a drop of water should sizzle and evaporate quickly).
Add high-heat oil and swirl to coat.

steps.title: Sear the first side
instructions: Lay steaks in the pan and press lightly to maximize contact.
Sear without moving for 2–3 minutes until a deep brown crust forms.

steps.title: Flip and sear the second side
instructions: Flip steaks and sear 2–3 minutes on the second side.
Sear edges briefly (fat cap) if applicable.

steps.title: Baste with garlic-herb butter
instructions: Add butter, smashed garlic, and herb sprigs to the pan.
Tilt the pan and spoon the foaming butter over the steaks continuously for 1–2 minutes.
Check internal temperature if using a thermometer and continue basting until you reach your target.
checks: Butter is foaming, not burning (if browning too fast, lower heat)

steps.title: Rest, then slice
instructions: Transfer steaks to a cutting board and rest 8–10 minutes.
Spoon a little garlic-herb butter over the top while resting.
Slice against the grain and serve. Optional: finish with a squeeze of lemon.

batchNotes: Cook in batches if pan is crowded; overcrowding prevents crust.
Make extra garlic-herb butter and use for vegetables or potatoes.

notes: Pull steak ~5°F before target; it rises while resting.
Times vary by thickness and pan heat—temperature is most reliable.

refrigerator.guidance: Cool and store airtight. Slice cold for sandwiches/salads or reheat gently.

freezer.guidance: Freezing cooked steak is possible but quality drops. Freeze sliced, tightly wrapped.

best: Warm slices briefly in a skillet with a splash of butter/broth
Warm whole steak in a low oven (250°F) then quick sear

summary: Pan-seared steak basted with foaming garlic-herb butter for a rich Herb & Garlic finish—temperature-driven for consistent doneness.

handling: Use tongs and avoid splatter; hot oil can burn.
Rest steak to allow juices to redistribute; do not leave cooked steak at room temperature longer than 2 hours.

variations.label: Reverse sear for thick steaks (1.5–2 inches)

notes: Bake steaks on a rack at 250°F until 10–15°F below target, then sear hot 60–90 seconds per side.
Finish with garlic-herb butter basting in the last minute.
Replace butter with 2 tbsp olive oil + 1 tbsp extra oil for basting.
Add minced garlic at the very end (off heat) to avoid burning.
Grill over high heat to desired doneness, then brush with garlic-herb butter off heat.
Make garlic-herb butter separately and spoon on top while resting.
